Prepare the liquidation reports for completed projects and effect necessary adjustments to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements and finally, abide by the terms and conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement between the source and recipient agencies to avail of the maximum benefit from the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleaning of PPE account balances of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Members of the Inventory Committee were absorbed of their regular duties aside from conforming and adjusting to new rules and regulations prescribed by the new administration which assumed office noon of June 30, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Office and other implementing offices to review the composition of the continuing 20% Development Fund, identify the savings of completed projects and those which were no longer prioritized and, subject to the full provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC, re-appropriate to current/existing priority development projects of the 20% DF. We also recommend that henceforth the MPDO and Municipal Engineer’s Office coordinate and strategize to optimally utilize the 20% DF; and regularly monitor the status of utilization and implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired an Engineer II to focus on the implementation of the program. Members of the Project Monitoring Committee were convened. However, some projects are not funded by the 20% DF causing delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱5,000.00 take home pay per month for them to maintain self-respect and dignity. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refrain from certifying for any loan applications should an employee’s net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that employees would want to receive the minimum of ₱5,000.00 take home pay per month to maintain self-respect and dignity but they also need to pay necessities like medicines, tuition fees and other expenses thus they sometimes apply for loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management prioritize the completion of the project so that it could be usable and beneficial to the PWDs. Target operation of the lift has to be made before the warranty period expires to ensure workability of the machine.

We recommend that Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct on time the physical inventory of all its property, plant and equipment, and prepare and submit the RPCCPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year, in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee has to reconcile the balance of PPE per books with that of the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records.
observations.managementAction: Members of the Inventory Committee were absorbed of their regular duties aside from conforming and adjusting to new rules and regulations prescribed by the new administration which assumed office noon of June 30, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, in order to fairly present the account Due from Local Government Units in the financial statements and provide proof that the purpose for the fund transfer has already been attained.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to all concerned barangays. All barangays except one with unliquidated financial assistance as of year 2021 have implemented their PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management evaluate whether the completion of the Desalination Project is still practical. Henceforth, strictly abide by the provisions of Item 1 of Annex “A” of 2016 RIRR of RA 9184. Feasibility study and detailed engineering should be conducted first before implementing any project.
observations.managementAction: The Engineering Office conducted an assessment to determine the current status of the project and submitted the report to LCE and Municipal Accountant. The amount of ₱6,000,000.00 was appropriated in 2023 for the completion of the Desalination project. Another ₱6,000,000.00 is also appropriated in 2024 for the proposed solar power system to be used for the power requirement of the Desalination plant. Design computation for the upgrading of the capacity of the plant has already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management require the immediate liquidation of fund transfer through demand letters signed by him or his authorized representative and to monitor/follow-up compliance thereof. Otherwise, Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to all concerned barangays. All barangays which were given financial assistance in the year 2019 have already submitted their liquidation reports to COA. However, the NGO PADAYON Bohol Marine Triangle Management Council, Inc. have not yet submitted their liquidation report to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling-up of the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct on time the physical inventory of all its property, plant and equipment, and prepare and submit the RPCCPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year, in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume I. We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record transactions to its appropriate account as provided in the PPSAS Chart of Accounts in order to accurately present the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate that charges to the General Fund for payment of taxes be replenished immediately to avoid depletion of the general fund.  Further, require the personnel concerned to trace the transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account to the accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments and take extra care in preparation of checks for payment to avoid erroneous charging of transactions.

We recommend that the management require the Municipal Accountant to revert the accounts payable without valid supporting documents and outstanding for more than two years to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD 1445 and DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-6.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, in order to fairly present the account Due from Local Government Units in the financial statements and provide proof that the purpose for the fund transfer has already been attained.

We recommend that management coordinate with the source agencies of the financial assistance and inquire if the funds were recorded as receivables or as donations in their books. If proven to be receivables, we recommend that the funds be returned because the purpose for which the fund was given is no longer feasible.  On the other hand, if these are donated funds, pursue the authorization by passing an ordinance so it can be utilized for projects and programs that would benefit the constituents.

We recommend that management require the Disbursing Officer to maintain two (2) separate cashbooks per fund and segregate the recording/posting of transactions of Advances for Payroll and Advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings to facilitate the reconciliation with the accounting records.  Further, we also recommend that reconciliation of accounting records and the cashbook be done at least quarterly.
We recommend that management require the BAC Secretariat to observe the mandatory posting of all notices and contracts awarded pursuant to the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit a supplemental bid/bulletin, if any, showing the change in the date of delivery from 30 days to 60 days. Moreover, it is recommended that the BAC issue a Supplemental/Bid Bulletin for any clarification/modification to any provisions of the Bidding Documents.

We recommend that management fast track the implementation of development projects to attain the desired benefits of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1. Henceforth, the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineering Office properly plan the scheduling and prioritization of development projects and closely monitor their implementation to ensure timely completion.

We recommend that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper accounts are used in recording transactions as guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2015-009 to prevent inaccurate and misleading information pursuant to Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend further that Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables to arrive at the correct amounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to:
a.	require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary accounting entries to book up the unrecognized PPE amounting ₱6,077,219.65 in the books and provide its corresponding depreciation expense and to establish and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards; and,
b.	require the designated Property Custodian to establish and maintain property cards for each class of PPE, to complete the physical inventory taking and to prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of PPE.

We recommend that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper accounts are used in recording transactions as guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2015-009 to prevent inaccurate and misleading information pursuant to Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the following measures be undertaken by the Management:
1.	Submit an inventory report on the salvaged materials from the demolished isolation/quarantine facility for proper accounting;
2.	Submit explanation/comment for non-filing of application for disposal/divestment of the isolation/quarantine facility to the Commission; and,
3.	Submit copy of resolution/authorization from the Sangguinang Bayan.
Finally, all disposal of supplies and properties should conform to Rule 22 or the Reporting of Disposable Supplies and Property under COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer record in chronological order all cash advances and liquidations made in her cashbook and rule and foot this at the end of the month to be able to ascertain the balances of her accountabilities and reconcile it with the general ledger.
We recommend that Management create a Citizen’s Charter Task Force composed of the department heads and facilitate the updating of the Citizen’s Charter.

We recommend that Management create an Internal Audit Service (IAS) Unit that would handle internal control functions of the agency pursuant to the above-mentioned rules and regulations. Refer to the PGIAM and the NGICS as guide in strengthening the internal control system of the LGU.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with rules and regulations and rates of allowances for claims of expenses for official travel prescribed under EO No. 77 to ensure economical and prudent use of government funds.  Further, require the official concerned to refund the excess of the maximum allowable hotel accommodation pursuant to the aforecited Executive Order.

We recommend that the BAC has to review meticulously all the contents and details of the bidding documents before posting the bid opportunity to prevent any discrepancies in the procurement process.  Observe strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that Management evaluate whether the completion of the Desalination Project is still practical.
Henceforth, strictly abide by the provisions of Item 1 of Annex “A” of 2016 RIRR of RA 9184. Feasibility study and detailed engineering should be conducted first before implementing any project.

We recommend that management provide a written explanation or provide additional documentary requirement on this noted observation. Moreover, we recommend that the BAC strictly adhere to the GPPB Circulars and Advisories for procurement procedures and requirements.

We recommend that Management provide a written explanation to justify the non-posting in the GPPB Online Portal the appropriate documents relative to the procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.

We recommend that management ensure that distribution of food packs is properly accounted and documented. Further, we require submission of the distribution list showing the contents of the packs for the beneficiaries.  Henceforth, ensure that utilization/disbursement of funds from the Bayanihan grant is in accordance with Item 3.10 of DBM LBC No. 125 to establish the propriety, validity and reasonableness of the transactions.
We recommend that the Local School Board present the CY 2019 SEF Budget in accordance with Section 5.2.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board presented the SEF Budget by program, activity and project (PAP), by allotment class and by school starting CY 2020 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management require BAC to submit and post the approved PMR for CY 2019 in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman submitted PMR of CY 2019 to GPPB thru email.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management direct the:

a. The Acting Municipal Engineer to issue Certificates of Completion of these projects as basis by the Municipal Accountant in the reclassification to their proper PPE account; and,

b. The Municipal Accountant to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of these projects in accordance with IPSAS 17.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management maximize the utilization of the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities to efficiently manage and address eventualities for risks or emergencies due to occurrence of man-made or natural calamities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause for the insurance of all insurable physical assets of the municipality with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS. observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the continuing projects of the 20% development fund. observations.recommendation:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen the internal control system in hiring of additional workforce through job orders and observe related laws and regulations enumerated above. Recommendations:

Initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties  by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality;
Require the Inventory Committee  to conduct the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement.

The Office of the Municipal Treasurer should request additional staff for the conduct of the physical inventory.

Comply to Section 489 of GAAM Volume I by securing insurance coverage, for all property which the LGU has insurable interest. The property insurance would assume recovery of cost of the property in case of loss.

Facilitate turn-over of documents of multi-cabs issued to barangays so that so that     so that these will be recorded in the books of the barangays and LTO registration of the same shall be shouldered by the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Insurance coverage for other insurable buildings shall be secured in the ensuing year except for the public market which is uninsurable according to GSIS standards.  Although the multicabs are recorded in the LGU books, LTO registration of the same are shouldered by the barangays. We have already requested our SB to pass a resolution to facilitate  the donation of the multicabs to the barangays.  We will also comply with the requirements needed for the transfer of ownership.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant attach the necessary documents/schedules/computations to support all the transactions recorded in the General Journal taking up the Depreciation Expenses.
observations.managementAction: Lapsing schedule had been submitted by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
Use the biometrics time clock in the daily time records of employees in order to reflect the actual hours rendered in the office and to control/monitor the attendance of all government employees. 

Monitor all employees’ attendance in their respective work areas to   ensure that all services due to the public are properly rendered.

c. Monitor and record all undertimes and tardiness in the DTRs to support salaries/wages claims.
observations.managementAction: Biometric machine has been fully utilized since August 1, 2017 in monitoring actual hours rendered  in the office by permanent employees.  Another biometric machine for job order employees will be purchased this 2018.  Using of pass slips to monitor employees time out from the office on official business has been implemented as well.  Leaves of absence, undertimes and tardiness has been properly monitored.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the municipal accountant.  Based on       the list the municipal accountant shall prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant set the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable based on the Assessment Roll of the Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs.
observations.managementAction: Require the Local Council for the Protection of Children and the Office concerned [MSWDO] to formulate an action plan. Pass a Supplemental Budget for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the continuing projects of the 20% development fund.  Further, projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials.
observations.managementAction: Require the MPDC Officer and Municipal Engineer to prioritize project implementation of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
observations.managementAction: The management will revise the LDRRM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of P4,000 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.
observations.managementAction: Schedule a Re-orientation of Good Moral and Right Conduct to all regular employees and incorporate the discussion regarding provisions of Section 47 of the GAA Fiscal Year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause for the insurance of all insurable physical assets of the municipality with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS. Likewise, ensure that funds are allocated for the purpose so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated for any damage or loss arising from the risk the properties were insured for.
observations.managementAction: Secure insurance for all insurable assets.  Pass a Supplemental Budget in case funds allocated is not enough to pay for the insurance premium.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to identify programs, projects and activities funded by GAD funds that will address gender issues and concerns such as promotion of women’s rights, women’s empowerment and gender equality.
observations.managementAction: Require the members of the Gender & Development Council and the GAD Focal Person to acquire trainings on the identification of GAD issues and the formulation of a comprehensive GAD Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit a justification for failing to undergo competitive bidding in the purchase of gasoline, follow the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 as to when to resort to different modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Require the Bids and Awards Committee will conduct a thorough study on Guidelines on Index-Based Pricing for Procurement of Petroleum, Oil and Lubricant Product as prescribed by GPPB Resolution 05-2018 and to come up with a special plan for the procurement of fuel and lubricants.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to instruct all unit heads to include office supplies/materials as one of the items in their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for consolidation and preparation of the APP.  Likewise, the Budget Officer would look into its compliance prior to the approval of the APP.  The BAC has to secure copy of the APP as guide in the conduct of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee will implement the public bidding for Commonly used Office Supplies and Equipment as soon as the LGUs CY 2019 Annual Budget be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track the installation of other facilities needed in the project completion and request the contractor to run the project as contracted even beyond the contract duration.  In case of hesitation from the contractor, revisit the provisions of the contract and on the process of project implementation and hold the persons responsible for the delay accountable for the damages that may cause the government in case failure of implementation occurs.
observations.managementAction: New genset already set-up and running.  Letter was sent to the contractor last November 2017 requesting their company to complete and test run the desalination project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the excess amount in the CY 2014 PEI be refunded by the officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the granting of PEI for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill up or indicate the complete data or information on the Driver’s Trip Ticket.

Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.

 c. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.

d. Further advice all Leagues through its officers to purchase and use the Official Receipt of  Republic of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Accepted league receipts upon payment of dues, fees & etc.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the LGU.  Moreover, require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.  Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to update/adjust the Land account balance in the books based on the results of the identification of municipal-owned lots and its titling.
observations.managementAction: Inquired lot status from DENR Region VII (Verified which lots were titled and not titled)
observations.reason: Some files were missing

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer submits daily the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and its supporting documents to Accounting Unit so that the General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers will be updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information.
observations.managementAction: Contact suppliers upon availability of our check payment to expedite submission of RCI with supporting documents to Accounting Unit
observations.reason: Some suppliers can't claim their checks immediately causing delay in submitting the RCI and paid vouchers to the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant cause the remittance of barangay shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects through the use of such funds.
observations.managementAction: We will effect the transfer of the prior year's barangay shares of RPT this coming January.
observations.reason: We are still tracing prior years entries on RPT since balance per books does not tally with balance to be remitted to barangays.  Summary of RPT's for remittance to barangays prepared by Municipal Treasurer's Office is not balanced with the total Barangays share from collections for the month which causes difficulty on the part of the one in charge at the Accounting Office to effect a timely remittance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management compute/deduct liquidated damages equivalent to the number of days the project had been delayed from its payment to the contractor.

We also recommend that henceforth, management prohibits the extension of contract time due to (a) ordinary unfavorable weather conditions and (b) inexcusable failure or negligence of contractor to provide the required equipment, supplies or materials pursuant to Item 10.2, Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs.

We recommend that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with Sections 3.1 and 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and Section 335 of RA 7160 for the prevention of the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures.

We recommend that management issue demand letters to all concerned officers and employees, requiring immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, we recommend that management withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the continuing projects of the 20% development fund.  Further, projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials.
We recommend that management closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.

We recommend that management carefully review the budget/appropriation charged to SEF with emphasis on the expenses enumerated under Section 3.2 of the Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the DEPED, DBM and DILG.

We recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No.94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to source agencies or seek authority for the utilization of the excess funds.
We recommend that management monitor and demand the liquidation of this assistance so that the release of the new financial assistance will not be in delay.

Direct the Property Officer to document the application for disposal of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of P4,000 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause for the insurance of all insurable physical assets of the municipality with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS. Likewise, ensure that funds are allocated for the purpose so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated for any damage or loss arising from the risk the properties were insured for.

We recommend management to identify programs, projects and activities funded by GAD funds that will address gender issues and concerns such as promotion of women’s rights, women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit a justification for failure to undergo competitive bidding in the purchase of gasoline, follow the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in case alternative mode of procurement be resorted instead of public bidding.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman and procurement officials explain/justify the non-compliance with the provisions of R.A. 9184 on public bidding and using the Shopping mode of Procurement without complying with the conditions set and henceforth, officials concerned undertake all procurements by means of public bidding unless the alternative mode is warranted.

We recommend that management submit explanation on the non-submission of the perfected contract. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management acted on the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend further that Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables to arrive at the correct amounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: Presently preparing a summary of RPT and SET receivables. However, we would like to know if it would be possible to forego with the certified list  and use the system generated summary of RPT and SET receivables for the year coming from the program in the Assessor's Office.
observations.reason: The ledger cards are already updated.  However, Municipal Treasurer has to prepare a summary and submit it to the Municipal Accountant to have it as the basis in recording RPT receivable and Special Education Tax receivable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the municipal treasurer/general services officer as the case maybe, to religiously follow the procedures of inspection, acceptance and recording of deliveries and their subsequent issuances to end-users. 

Justify the violation of the basic procedures of inspection and acceptance.
observations.managementAction: Management acted on the recommendation

observations.managementAction: Biometric machine has been fully utilized since August 1, 2017.  However, one unit is not sufficient to accommodate (240) employees, both permanent and job order so that the biometric machine has been utilized for permanent employees only until another unit is available.  Puchase of one unit biometric machine will be done this second quarter of 2018.
observations.reason: Budget for a new biometric machine is available this year 2018 yet.
